title: problem with creation of pods via quadlet
body:
### Issue Description

for setting up a paperless-ngx server I have several .container files (one for each container). the setup depends on a pod.
with the following command everything works like a charm:
podman pod create --name ablage --infra-name ablage-pod -p 8000:8000

### Steps to reproduce the issue

using this pod file is not sufficient:
[Pod]
PodName=ablage
[Container]
AutoUpdate=registry
ContainerName=ablage.pod
PublishPort=8000:8000
[Service]
Restart=always
RestartSec=5

### Describe the results you received

the system does not start up

### Describe the results you expected

probably the "infra" container is missing. Is there an option to create the same result as the command mentioned above with a .pod file?
I do not find any how to in the docs.

### podman info output

```yaml
v5.4
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: Hi, not sure if I understand your files. Is what you wrote here a single file?

yes it is a single file: ablage.pod
```
[Pod]
PodName=ablage
[Container]
AutoUpdate=registry
ContainerName=ablage.pod
PublishPort=8000:8000
[Service]
Restart=always
RestartSec=5
```
my problem is that the command: podman pod create --name ablage --infra-name ablage-pod -p 8000:8000
does create a pod and an infra-container.
I would like to get the same result with the file definition. I do not find any example or hint how to do this in the docs.

Your `.pod` file is incorrect.

1. `.pod` files should only include a `[Pod]` section (with optional other systemd sections like `[Service]`). 
2. When using pods (instead of standalone containers), `PublishPort` belongs to the `[Pod]` section
3. `AutoUpdate` is not a `[Pod]` key
4. I guess you tried to control the name of the pod infra container name using `ContainerName`. First, this is currently not supported. But, even if it did, it would have belonged to the `[Pod]` section.

So, IIUC `ablage.pod` should be:
```
[Pod]
PodName=ablage
PublishPort=8000:8000

[Service]
Restart=always
RestartSec=5
```

It works - thank you very very much!!!
Thanks for your explanation.
For anyone who might be interested: I have to start the pod service first, then the webserver.

> For anyone who might be interested: I have to start the pod service first, then the webserver.

This should not happen. Quadlet should be able to set the dependencies. Are you referencing the pod's Quadlet unit in the webserver's `.container` file:
```
Pod=ablage.pod
```

I missed the .pod ...
thanks again - podman actually works great
so I can set it up with ansible...
